normalized_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ e ∈ edges, e.src < n ∧ e.dst < n ∧ (0 ≤ e.weight)
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: All directed edges stay within vertex bounds [0,n) and all edge weights are non-negative.
The returned distance vector has exactly n entries (one per vertex).
The source vertex is reachable with distance 0.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ v < n, dist[v] = some d → ∃ path, path.Source = source ∧ path.Target = v ∧ path.weight = d
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: If dist[v] is Some(d), then there exists a path from source to v whose total weight is exactly d.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ v < n, dist[v] = some d → ¬ (∃ d' < d, ∃ path, path.Source = source ∧ path.Target = v ∧ path.weight = d')
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: If dist[v] is Some(d), then no path from source to v has total weight strictly less than d.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ v < n, dist[v] = none → ¬ (∃ path, path.Source = source ∧ path.Target = v)
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: If dist[v] is None, then v is unreachable from the source.
The Dijkstra loop terminates for every valid input.
